title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework that simplifies GPU cloud deployment from IDEs, replacing multi-step Docker and registry workflows with a single @flash.endpoint annotation. It supports hot reload for model swaps and integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud infrastructure including serverless autoscaling across data centers.
writeup: This talk from RunPod's Audry Hsu introduces Flash, a decorator-based framework for deploying GPU workloads to the cloud directly from an IDE. It replaces the usual multi-step Docker build and registry push workflow with a single @flash.endpoint annotation, and it supports hot reload so models can be swapped without a full redeploy cycle. Flash integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud, including serverless autoscaling across data centers. The takeaway: the friction of containerizing and shipping GPU code is collapsed into an annotation plus the existing development loop.
